title: norna borealis var. europaea
intro: Norna borealis var. europaea, also known as European mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe, characterized by its white flowers and red berries. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Norna borealis var. europaea include reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant's bark, leaves, and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
